chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT MIRPURKHAS Constitutional Petition No.S-46 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For hearing of main case. 16-03-2026. Mr. Jeeloji Rajput advocate for the petitioner a/w petitioner. Mr. Shanker Lal advocate for respondent a/w respondent. Mr. Ayaz Ali Rajpar, A.A.G. = In compliance to last Order dated 10-03-2026, petitioner is present in person alongwith minor and meeting of the minor with the respondent/ father has been conducted in the Court. Learned counsel(s) for both the parties were also heard at length. The petitioner’s claims that the Decree of both the learned Courts below have not been challenged by the respondent and he has not complied with the Decree(s) and has only deposited Rs.4000/= on account of maintenance of the minor so far. On query, about the maintenance amount of the minor, the respondent has handed over cash amount of Rs.20,000/= to the petitioner in the Court through her counsel and he undertakes to deposit the remaining decreetal amount, if reasonable time is allowed to him. At this stage, after arguing the matter at length, both the learned counsel(s) of the parties, under instructions of their respective client, state that they would be satisfied if both the Decrees passed by the learned Courts below are partly upheld; and the petitioner be allowed to produce further evidence, if any, with respect to her claim of dowry articles before the learned trial Court; and three months’ time is allowed to the respondent to deposit the outstanding decreetal amount so far Rs. 60,000/=, in three equal installments of Rs.20,000/= each; i.e. first installment shall be deposited on or before 16-04-2026, 2nd installment shall be deposited on or before 16-05-2026 and third installment shall be deposited on or before 16-06-2026.

On query, about the maintenance amount of the minor, the respondent has handed over cash amount of Rs.20,000/= to the petitioner in the Court through her counsel and he undertakes to deposit the remaining decreetal amount, if reasonable time is allowed to him. At this stage, after arguing the matter at length, both the learned counsel(s) of the parties, under instructions of their respective client, state that they would be satisfied if both the Decrees passed by the learned Courts below are partly upheld; and the petitioner be allowed to produce further evidence, if any, with respect to her claim of dowry articles before the learned trial Court; and three months’ time is allowed to the respondent to deposit the outstanding decreetal amount so far Rs. 60,000/=, in three equal installments of Rs.20,000/= each; i.e. first installment shall be deposited on or before 16-04-2026, 2nd installment shall be deposited on or before 16-05-2026 and third installment shall be deposited on or before 16-06-2026. 2 Order accordingly. The instant Petition is, therefore, partly allowed, to the extent that the impugned Judgment(s) and Decree(s) of the learned lower Courts are upheld to the extent of maintenance of the minor and dower amount, which shall be paid by the respondent in the manner as recorded in the preceding paragraph; whereas with respect to petitioner’s claim for dowry articles, matter is remanded back to the learned trial Court to allow opportunity to petitioner to produce further evidence on the issue of her claim of dowry articles and then based upon evidence produced by both the parties on this issue only, to decide the said issue afresh. This Petition stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE *Saleem* 3
